summary: Clean, well-aligned DevOps resume that correctly avoids the forbidden fabricated metrics and stays within the source-of-truth facts; only minor polish and a couple of medium JD-alignment notes.

issues.issue: Summary omits the misleading '82% (45→8 min)' claim from the original cv.md — the generated version correctly reframes to legitimate metrics (cost 30%, uptime 99.9%, toil 70%). This is a POSITIVE correction, noted here only to confirm compliance with the source-of-truth rule.
issues.where: summary: 'cutting compute cost 30% ($500+/month), sustaining 99.9% uptime ... eliminating ~70% of operational toil'
issues.suggestion: No change needed — this is compliant with the master brief's prohibition on the 82%/45→8min and 80%/95% drift claims.

issues.issue: The Healthcare project bullets correctly drop the prototype-scale outcomes ('500+ concurrent users', '30% pull latency', '5+ IAM roles removed') per the master brief warning — good. However confirm 'zero-downtime redeployment' is defensible for a prototype; the brief frames it as 'demonstrating zero-downtime redeployment for client review', which matches, so it is acceptable.
issues.where: Healthcare project: 'demonstrating zero-downtime redeployment for client review'
issues.suggestion: Leave as-is; it stays at prototype-demonstration scale and avoids production claims. No fabrication present.

issues.issue: colorScheme is 'teal' (startup/SaaS scheme). Master brief default for Cloud/SRE/enterprise is Navy, but Lucidity is a cloud-cost-optimization SaaS startup, so teal is arguably appropriate. Minor — verify intent.
issues.suggestion: Teal is fine for a SaaS/startup like Lucidity; no change required.

issues.issue: Lucidity's core product is autonomous cloud storage/cost optimization (block storage auto-scaling, EBS cost reduction). The resume's genuine 30% compute-cost-reduction and Karpenter/KEDA/CronJob cost work is strongly relevant and well-surfaced, but the summary could lead harder on 'cloud cost optimization' as a headline theme to match Lucidity's mission.
issues.suggestion: Consider explicitly using the phrase 'cloud cost optimization' in the summary to mirror Lucidity's product domain and boost ATS/keyword match.

issues.issue: No target job responsibilities/keywords were provided beyond title/company, so JD keyword-gap analysis is limited. Based on Lucidity's known domain, EBS/storage-cost and observability terms are already present.
issues.suggestion: If the full JD lists specific tools (e.g., specific monitoring or storage tech), tailor a skill row to match; otherwise current alignment is solid.

issues.issue: Contact line (phone, email, LinkedIn, GitHub, location) is present in cv.md but not reproduced in the JSON payload — assuming the renderer injects the header from profile.yml. Confirm the header renders all five contact fields.
issues.where: Generated JSON has no explicit header/contact block
issues.suggestion: Ensure the HTML renderer pulls name, phone, email, LinkedIn, GitHub, and location into the header; all values exist in profile.yml.

issues.issue: Spelling of 'utilization'/'authoring' uses US -ize/-ize forms while 'Dockerised' uses British -ise; the master brief mandates British -ise. Mixed convention.
issues.suggestion: For full consistency with the brief's British -ise rule, use 'utilisation'. Minor and ATS-neutral.

issues.issue: Achievement 'Student of the Year in Class 10' is weak/dated for a DevOps role but is the only legitimate achievement available per the brief.
issues.where: achievements: 'Awarded "Student of the Year" in Class 10'
issues.suggestion: Acceptable given no other achievements exist; keep only if space allows, otherwise the Achievements section could be dropped to save one-page room.

issues.issue: Bullets are strong, quantified, and lead with action verbs (Reduced, Built, Operated, Maintained, Drove, Hardened). No fluff or duty-only lines detected.
issues.suggestion: No change — impact quality is high.
